content:
# Global Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent running in a headless orchestration session.
There is no human in the loop — do not ask questions or wait for input.

## Ground rules

1. Read and follow the project's CLAUDE.md for coding conventions and standards.
2. Never use interactive commands, slash commands, or plan mode.
3. Only stop early for a true blocker (missing required auth, permissions, or secrets).
   If blocked, post the blocker details as a Linear comment and stop.
4. Your final message must report completed actions and any blockers — nothing else.

## Execution approach

- Spend extra effort on planning and verification.
- Read all relevant files before writing code.
- When planning: read CLAUDE.md, the existing code in the area you are modifying, and any related docs.
- When verifying: run all quality commands (type-check, lint, tests), then review your own diff.
- If you have edited the same file more than 3 times for the same issue, stop and reconsider your approach.

## Session startup

Before starting any implementation work:

1. Run the project's type-check command to verify the codebase compiles clean.
2. Run the project's test command to verify all tests pass.
3. If either fails, investigate and fix before starting new work.

## Linear workpad

Use a single Linear comment as a persistent workpad:

- Title: `## Workpad`
- Update it at each milestone with: current status, decisions made, and next steps.
- On rework runs, append the rework section — do not delete prior content.

## Rework awareness

Every prompt in this workflow serves both first-run and rework cases.
On rework runs, the workspace already contains prior work.  Check for:

- An existing feature branch (do not create a new one)
- An open PR (push to it, do not open a second)
- Review comments requesting changes (address them specifically)
- Prior workpad content (append to it, do not overwrite)

# Investigation Stage

You are investigating issue ****: 

**Current status:** 
**Labels:** 
**URL:** 

## Issue description


No description provided.


## Objective

Understand the problem thoroughly before any code is written.  Your output is
an investigation summary posted as a Linear comment — not code changes.

## First run

1. Read the issue description and any existing Linear comments.
2. Identify the relevant source files — read them, understand the architecture.
3. If the issue is a bug: reproduce it first (run the failing test or repro steps).
4. If the issue is a feature: map out which files/modules need changes.
5. Write a structured investigation summary:
   - **Root cause** or **Requirements** (depending on issue type)
   - **Affected files** with brief explanation of needed changes
   - **Risks or open questions**
   - **Proposed approach** (high-level, 3-5 bullet points)
6. Post the summary as a Linear comment titled `## Investigation`.
7. Update the workpad with investigation status.

## Rework run

If this is a rework run (the workspace already has investigation content):

1. Read the review feedback from Linear comments.
2. Read your prior investigation summary.
3. Address the specific feedback — expand analysis, correct mistakes, or
   investigate additional areas as requested.
4. Update the `## Investigation` comment with revised findings.
5. Append a rework note to the workpad.

## Do NOT

- Write implementation code.
- Create branches or PRs.
- Modify source files (reading is fine).

---
<!-- AUTO-GENERATED BY STOKOWSKI — DO NOT EDIT -->

## Lifecycle Context

- **Issue:** CHA-13 — P1.6: Implement CrmService for live store
- **URL:** https://linear.app/chaimisu/issue/CHA-13/p16-implement-crmservice-for-live-store
- **State:** investigate
- **Run:** 1

### Transitions

- `complete` → **research-review**

### When Done

When you have completed your work, post a summary comment on the Linear issue describing what was done and any decisions made.

<!-- END STOKOWSKI LIFECYCLE -->

content.thinking: Let me investigate the CRM crate and understand what's needed for implementing CrmService for a live store. I need to explore the codebase first.

content.text: Let me start by exploring the CRM-related code and understanding the current architecture.

commands.label: CRM all source files
commands.command: for f in $(find crates/crm/src -name '*.rs' | sort); do echo '===== '$f' ====='; cat $f; done

commands.label: CRM migrations
commands.command: find crates/crm/migrations -type f | sort && for f in $(find crates/crm/migrations -type f | sort); do echo '===== '$f' ====='; cat $f; done

commands.label: CRM config schema
commands.command: grep -n -A 30 'crm' crates/config/src/schema.rs || echo 'no crm in schema'

commands.label: Server startup CRM
commands.command: grep -n 'crm' crates/gateway/src/server.rs || echo 'no crm in server.rs'

queries: How is CRM integrated into the gateway server startup
CRM database tables and migrations schema
What CRM models exist (Contact, Matter, Interaction)
How does the config schema define CRM settings
Any existing live store vs mock store pattern in the codebase

1→//! CRM (Contact Relationship Management) for moltis.
     2→//!
     3→//! Provides domain types and persistent stores for tracking people and entities
     4→//! that interact with the system across any channel.
     5→//!
     6→//! # Core types
     7→//!
     8→//! - [`Contact`] — a person or entity, optionally with PII fields (`email`, `phone`)
     9→//! - [`ContactChannel`] — a channel identity linking a contact to an external platform
    10→//! - [`Matter`] — a legal case or work item linked to a contact
    11→//! - [`Interaction`] — a recorded touchpoint between the system and a contact
    12→//!
    13→//! # Enums
    14→//!
    15→//! - [`ContactStage`] — contact lifecycle stage
    16→//! - [`MatterStatus`] / [`MatterPhase`] — matter lifecycle state and phase
    17→//! - [`PracticeArea`] — legal practice area
    18→//! - [`InteractionKind`] — type of interaction
    19→
    20→pub mod error;
    21→pub mod store;
    22→pub mod store_memory;
    23→pub mod store_sqlite;
    24→pub mod types;
    25→
    26→pub use {
    27→    error::{Error, Result},
    28→    store::{CrmStore, MemoryCrmStore, SqliteCrmStore},
    29→    types::{
    30→        Contact, ContactChannel, ContactStage, Interaction, InteractionKind, Matter, MatterPhase,
    31→        MatterStatus, PracticeArea,
    32→    },
    33→};
    34→
    35→/// Run database migrations for the CRM crate.
    36→///
    37→/// Creates and updates the `crm_contacts`, `crm_matters`, `crm_interactions`,
    38→/// and `crm_contact_channels` tables with indexes. Should be called at
    39→/// application startup before using [`SqliteCrmStore`].
    40→pub async fn run_migrations(pool: &sqlx::SqlitePool) -> Result<()> {
    41→    sqlx::migrate!("./migrations")
    42→        .set_ignore_missing(true)
    43→        .run(pool)
    44→        .await?;
    45→    Ok(())
    46→}
    47→

1→use {
     2→    secrecy::{ExposeSecret, Secret},
     3→    serde::{Deserialize, Serialize},
     4→};
     5→
     6→// ── Serde helpers for Secret<String> ─────────────────────────────────────────
     7→//
     8→// `Secret<String>` requires the `SerializableSecret` marker for its Serialize
     9→// impl as a security guardrail. We use explicit helpers instead.
    10→
    11→fn serialize_option_secret<S>(
    12→    secret: &Option<Secret<String>>,
    13→    serializer: S,
    14→) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error>
    15→where
    16→    S: serde::Serializer,
    17→{
    18→    match secret {
    19→        Some(s) => serializer.serialize_some(s.expose_secret()),
    20→        None => serializer.serialize_none(),
    21→    }
    22→}
    23→
    24→fn deserialize_option_secret<'de, D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Option<Secret<String>>, D::Error>
    25→where
    26→    D: serde::Deserializer<'de>,
    27→{
    28→    let opt: Option<String> = Option::deserialize(deserializer)?;
    29→    Ok(opt.map(Secret::new))
    30→}
    31→
    32→// ── Contact stage ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    33→
    34→/// Lifecycle stage of a contact.
    35→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
    36→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    37→pub enum ContactStage {
    38→    /// Initial outreach — not yet engaged.
    39→    #[default]
    40→    Lead,
    41→    /// Engaged but no active matter.
    42→    Prospect,
    43→    /// Has an open matter.
    44→    Active,
    45→    /// Previously active, currently dormant.
    46→    Inactive,
    47→    /// Relationship closed.
    48→    Closed,
    49→}
    50→
    51→impl ContactStage {
    52→    #[must_use]
    53→    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
    54→        match self {
    55→            Self::Lead => "lead",
    56→            Self::Prospect => "prospect",
    57→            Self::Active => "active",
    58→            Self::Inactive => "inactive",
    59→            Self::Closed => "closed",
    60→        }
    61→    }
    62→}
    63→
    64→impl std::fmt::Display for ContactStage {
    65→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
    66→        f.write_str(self.as_str())
    67→    }
    68→}
    69→
    70→impl std::str::FromStr for ContactStage {
    71→    type Err = String;
    72→
    73→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
    74→        match s {
    75→            "lead" => Ok(Self::Lead),
    76→            "prospect" => Ok(Self::Prospect),
    77→            "active" => Ok(Self::Active),
    78→            "inactive" => Ok(Self::Inactive),
    79→            "closed" => Ok(Self::Closed),
    80→            other => Err(format!("unknown ContactStage: {other:?}")),
    81→        }
    82→    }
    83→}
    84→
    85→// ── Matter status ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    86→
    87→/// Lifecycle status of a matter.
    88→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
    89→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    90→pub enum MatterStatus {
    91→    /// Matter is actively being worked on.
    92→    #[default]
    93→    Open,
    94→    /// Matter is temporarily paused.
    95→    OnHold,
    96→    /// Matter has been resolved.
    97→    Closed,
    98→    /// Matter is archived (read-only).
    99→    Archived,
   100→}
   101→
   102→impl MatterStatus {
   103→    #[must_use]
   104→    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
   105→        match self {
   106→            Self::Open => "open",
   107→            Self::OnHold => "on_hold",
   108→            Self::Closed => "closed",
   109→            Self::Archived => "archived",
   110→        }
   111→    }
   112→}
   113→
   114→impl std::fmt::Display for MatterStatus {
   115→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   116→        f.write_str(self.as_str())
   117→    }
   118→}
   119→
   120→impl std::str::FromStr for MatterStatus {
   121→    type Err = String;
   122→
   123→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
   124→        match s {
   125→            "open" => Ok(Self::Open),

   127→            "closed" => Ok(Self::Closed),
   128→            "archived" => Ok(Self::Archived),

   132→}

   134→// ── Matter phase ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   135→
   136→/// Phase within the lifecycle of a matter.
   137→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
   138→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]

   140→    /// Initial assessment and onboarding.
   141→    #[default]
   142→    Intake,
   143→    /// Gathering facts, evidence, or documents.
   144→    Discovery,
   145→    /// Active negotiation or dispute.
   146→    Negotiation,

   148→    Resolution,
   149→    /// Post-resolution review or appeal period.
   150→    Review,

   152→    Closed,
   153→}
   154→
   155→impl MatterPhase {
   156→    #[must_use]

   159→            Self::Intake => "intake",
   160→            Self::Discovery => "discovery",
   161→            Self::Negotiation => "negotiation",
   162→            Self::Resolution => "resolution",
   163→            Self::Review => "review",

   165→        }
   166→    }
   167→}
   168→

   173→}
   174→

   192→
   193→/// Practice area or domain of a matter.
   194→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]

   304→        match s {
   305→            "call" => Ok(Self::Call),
   306→            "email" => Ok(Self::Email),
   307→            "message" => Ok(Self::Message),
   308→            "meeting" => Ok(Self::Meeting),
   309→            "note" => Ok(Self::Note),
   310→            "document" => Ok(Self::Document),
   311→            other => Err(format!("unknown InteractionKind: {other:?}")),
   312→        }
   313→    }
   314→}
   315→
   316→// ── Contact ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   317→
   318→/// A contact in the CRM system.
   319→///
   320→/// Contacts represent people or entities that interact with the system through
   321→/// any channel (Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, etc.) or are imported from external
   322→/// sources.
   323→///
   324→/// PII fields (`email`, `phone`) are wrapped in [`Secret`] to prevent
   325→/// accidental exposure in logs and debug output.
   326→#[derive(Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   327→pub struct Contact {
   328→    /// Unique identifier (UUID v4).
   329→    pub id: String,
   330→    /// Human-readable display name.
   331→    pub name: String,
   332→    /// Origin channel or import source (e.g. `"telegram"`, `"whatsapp"`, `"csv-import"`).
   333→    #[serde(default)]
   334→    pub source: Option<String>,
   335→    /// Identifier assigned by the external channel (e.g. Telegram user ID).
   336→    #[serde(default)]
   337→    pub external_id: Option<String>,
   338→    /// Email address — PII, redacted in debug output.
   339→    #[serde(
   340→        default,
   341→        serialize_with = "serialize_option_secret",
   342→        deserialize_with = "deserialize_option_secret"
   343→    )]
   344→    pub email: Option<Secret<String>>,
   345→    /// Phone number — PII, redacted in debug output.
   346→    #[serde(
   347→        default,
   348→        serialize_with = "serialize_option_secret",
   349→        deserialize_with = "deserialize_option_secret"
   350→    )]
   351→    pub phone: Option<Secret<String>>,
   352→    /// Lifecycle stage of this contact.
   353→    #[serde(default)]
   354→    pub stage: ContactStage,
   355→    /// Arbitrary structured metadata stored as a JSON object.
   356→    #[serde(default)]
   357→    pub metadata: serde_json::Value,
   358→    /// Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the contact was created.
   359→    pub created_at: u64,
   360→    /// Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the contact was last updated.
   361→    pub updated_at: u64,
   362→}
   363→
   364→impl std::fmt::Debug for Contact {
   365→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   366→        f.debug_struct("Contact")
   367→            .field("id", &self.id)
   368→            .field("name", &self.name)
   369→            .field("source", &self.source)
   370→            .field("external_id", &self.external_id)
   371→            .field("email", &self.email.as_ref().map(|_| "[REDACTED]"))
   372→            .field("phone", &self.phone.as_ref().map(|_| "[REDACTED]"))
   373→            .field("stage", &self.stage)
   374→            .field("metadata", &self.metadata)
   375→            .field("created_at", &self.created_at)
   376→            .field("updated_at", &self.updated_at)
   377→            .finish()
   378→    }
   379→}
   380→
   381→impl PartialEq for Contact {
   382→    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
   383→        self.id == other.id
   384→            && self.name == other.name
   385→            && self.source == other.source
   386→            && self.external_id == other.external_id
   387→            && opt_secret_eq(&self.email, &other.email)
   388→            && opt_secret_eq(&self.phone, &other.phone)
   389→            && self.stage == other.stage
   390→            && self.metadata == other.metadata
   391→            && self.created_at == other.created_at
   392→            && self.updated_at == other.updated_at
   393→    }
   394→}
   395→
   396→fn opt_secret_eq(a: &Option<Secret<String>>, b: &Option<Secret<String>>) -> bool {
   397→    match (a, b) {
   398→        (None, None) => true,
   399→        (Some(x), Some(y)) => x.expose_secret() == y.expose_secret(),
   400→        _ => false,
   401→    }
   402→}
   403→
   404→impl Contact {
   405→    /// Create a new contact with the given name and auto-generated UUID.
   406→    #[must_use]
   407→    pub fn new(name: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
   408→        let now = now_ms();
   409→        Self {
   410→            id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),
   411→            name: name.into(),
   412→            source: None,
   413→            external_id: None,
   414→            email: None,
   415→            phone: None,
   416→            stage: ContactStage::default(),
   417→            metadata: serde_json::Value::Object(serde_json::Map::new()),
   418→            created_at: now,
   419→            updated_at: now,
   420→        }
   421→    }
   422→
   423→    /// Create a new contact with explicit source and external ID.
   424→    #[must_use]
   425→    pub fn with_source(
   426→        name: impl Into<String>,
   427→        source: impl Into<String>,
   428→        external_id: impl Into<String>,
   429→    ) -> Self {
   430→        let mut c = Self::new(name);
   431→        c.source = Some(source.into());
   432→        c.external_id = Some(external_id.into());
   433→        c
   434→    }
   435→}
   436→
   437→// ── ContactChannel ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   438→
   439→/// A channel identity record linking a contact to an external platform.
   440→///
   441→/// A single contact can have multiple channel identities (e.g., the same
   442→/// person reachable on Telegram, WhatsApp, and email). The `channel_type`
   443→/// field holds the channel identifier string (e.g., `"telegram"`, `"slack"`).
   444→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
   445→pub struct ContactChannel {
   446→    /// Unique identifier (UUID v4).
   447→    pub id: String,
   448→    /// Contact this channel belongs to.
   449→    pub contact_id: String,
   450→    /// Channel type identifier (e.g. `"telegram"`, `"whatsapp"`, `"email"`).
   451→    pub channel_type: String,
   452→    /// Channel-native identifier for this contact (e.g. Telegram user ID).
   453→    pub channel_id: String,
   454→    /// Human-readable display name on this channel (e.g. username, email address).
   455→    #[serde(default)]
   456→    pub display_name: Option<String>,
   457→    /// Whether this channel identity has been verified.
   458→    #[serde(default)]
   459→    pub verified: bool,
   460→    /// Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the record was created.
   461→    pub created_at: u64,
   462→    /// Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the record was last updated.
   463→    pub updated_at: u64,
   464→}
   465→
   466→impl ContactChannel {
   467→    /// Create a new channel identity for a contact.
   468→    #[must_use]
   469→    pub fn new(
   470→        contact_id: impl Into<String>,
   471→        channel_type: impl Into<String>,
   472→        channel_id: impl Into<String>,
   473→    ) -> Self {
   474→        let now = now_ms();
   475→        Self {
   476→            id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),
   477→            contact_id: contact_id.into(),
   478→            channel_type: channel_type.into(),
   479→            channel_id: channel_id.into(),
   480→            display_name: None,
   481→            verified: false,
   482→            created_at: now,
   483→            updated_at: now,
   484→        }
   485→    }
   486→}

   488→// ── Matter ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   489→
   490→/// A legal matter or case optionally linked to a contact.
   491→///
   492→/// `contact_id` is nullable: a matter may span multiple contacts or exist
   493→/// independently. When a contact is deleted the DB sets this to NULL via
   494→/// `ON DELETE SET NULL`.
   495→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
   496→pub struct Matter {
   497→    /// Unique identifier (UUID v4).
   498→    pub id: String,
   499→    /// Contact primarily associated with this matter, if any.
   500→    #[serde(default)]
   501→    pub contact_id: Option<String>,
   502→    /// Short descriptive title.
   503→    pub title: String,
   504→    /// Optional longer description.
   505→    #[serde(default)]
   506→    pub description: Option<String>,
   507→    /// Current lifecycle status.
   508→    #[serde(default)]
   509→    pub status: MatterStatus,
   510→    /// Current phase within the matter lifecycle.
   511→    #[serde(default)]
   512→    pub phase: MatterPhase,
   513→    /// Practice area or domain of this matter.
   514→    pub practice_area: PracticeArea,
   515→    /// Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the matter was created.
   516→    pub created_at: u64,
   517→    /// Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the matter was last updated.
   518→    pub updated_at: u64,
   519→}
   520→
   521→impl Matter {
   522→    /// Create a new matter linked to the given contact.
   523→    #[must_use]
   524→    pub fn new(
   525→        contact_id: impl Into<String>,
   526→        title: impl Into<String>,
   527→        practice_area: PracticeArea,
   528→    ) -> Self {
   529→        let now = now_ms();
   530→        Self {
   531→            id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),
   532→            contact_id: Some(contact_id.into()),
   533→            title: title.into(),
   534→            description: None,
   535→            status: MatterStatus::default(),
   536→            phase: MatterPhase::default(),
   537→            practice_area,
   538→            created_at: now,
   539→            updated_at: now,
   540→        }
   541→    }
   542→}
   543→
   544→// ── Interaction ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   545→
   546→/// A recorded touchpoint between the system and a contact.
   547→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
   548→pub struct Interaction {
   549→    /// Unique identifier (UUID v4).
   550→    pub id: String,
   551→    /// Contact involved in this interaction.
   552→    pub contact_id: String,
   553→    /// Optional matter this interaction relates to.
   554→    #[serde(default)]
   555→    pub matter_id: Option<String>,
   556→    /// Type of interaction.
   557→    pub kind: InteractionKind,
   558→    /// Summary of what occurred.
   559→    pub summary: String,
   560→    /// Optional channel where the interaction took place (e.g. `"telegram"`).
   561→    #[serde(default)]
   562→    pub channel: Option<String>,
   563→    /// Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the interaction was created.
   564→    pub created_at: u64,
   565→    /// Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the interaction was last updated.
   566→    pub updated_at: u64,
   567→}
   568→
   569→impl Interaction {
   570→    /// Create a new interaction for the given contact.
   571→    #[must_use]
   572→    pub fn new(
   573→        contact_id: impl Into<String>,
   574→        kind: InteractionKind,
   575→        summary: impl Into<String>,
   576→    ) -> Self {
   577→        let now = now_ms();
   578→        Self {
   579→            id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),
   580→            contact_id: contact_id.into(),
   581→            matter_id: None,
   582→            kind,
   583→            summary: summary.into(),
   584→            channel: None,
   585→            created_at: now,
   586→            updated_at: now,
   587→        }
   588→    }
   589→}
   590→
   591→// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   592→
   593→fn now_ms() -> u64 {
   594→    use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};
   595→    SystemTime::now()
   596→        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
   597→        .unwrap_or_default()
   598→        .as_millis() as u64
   599→}

1→use async_trait::async_trait;
     2→
     3→use crate::{
     4→    Result,
     5→    types::{Contact, ContactChannel, Interaction, Matter},
     6→};
     7→
     8→/// Persistence trait for CRM data.
     9→///
    10→/// Provides CRUD operations for [`Contact`], [`Matter`], [`Interaction`], and
    11→/// [`ContactChannel`] records. Implementations include [`SqliteCrmStore`] for
    12→/// production use and [`MemoryCrmStore`] for testing.
    13→#[async_trait]
    14→pub trait CrmStore: Send + Sync {
    15→    // ── Contacts ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16→
    17→    /// Return all contacts ordered by most-recently-updated first.
    18→    async fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<Contact>>;
    19→
    20→    /// Return a single contact by ID, or `None` if not found.
    21→    async fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Contact>>;
    22→
    23→    /// Look up a contact by source channel and external ID.
    24→    async fn get_by_external(&self, source: &str, external_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Contact>>;
    25→
    26→    /// Insert or update a contact (upsert by ID).
    27→    async fn upsert(&self, contact: Contact) -> Result<()>;
    28→
    29→    /// Delete a contact by ID. Returns `Ok(())` if not found (idempotent).
    30→    async fn delete(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()>;
    31→
    32→    // ── Contact channels ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    33→
    34→    /// Return all channel identities for a contact.
    35→    async fn list_channels_for_contact(&self, contact_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<ContactChannel>>;
    36→
    37→    /// Look up a channel identity by channel type and channel-native ID.
    38→    async fn get_channel_by_external(
    39→        &self,
    40→        channel_type: &str,
    41→        channel_id: &str,
    42→    ) -> Result<Option<ContactChannel>>;
    43→
    44→    /// Insert or update a channel identity (upsert by ID).
    45→    async fn upsert_channel(&self, channel: ContactChannel) -> Result<()>;
    46→
    47→    /// Delete a channel identity by ID. Returns `Ok(())` if not found (idempotent).
    48→    async fn delete_channel(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()>;
    49→
    50→    // ── Matters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    51→
    52→    /// Return all matters ordered by most-recently-updated first.
    53→    async fn list_matters(&self) -> Result<Vec<Matter>>;
    54→
    55→    /// Return all matters for a contact, ordered by most-recently-updated first.
    56→    async fn list_matters_by_contact(&self, contact_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Matter>>;
    57→
    58→    /// Return a single matter by ID, or `None` if not found.
    59→    async fn get_matter(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Matter>>;
    60→
    61→    /// Insert or update a matter (upsert by ID).
    62→    async fn upsert_matter(&self, matter: Matter) -> Result<()>;
    63→
    64→    /// Delete a matter by ID. Returns `Ok(())` if not found (idempotent).
    65→    async fn delete_matter(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()>;
    66→
    67→    // ── Interactions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    68→
    69→    /// Return all interactions for a contact, ordered by most-recently-updated first.
    70→    async fn list_interactions_by_contact(&self, contact_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Interaction>>;
    71→
    72→    /// Return all interactions for a matter, ordered by most-recently-updated first.
    73→    async fn list_interactions_by_matter(&self, matter_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Interaction>>;
    74→
    75→    /// Return a single interaction by ID, or `None` if not found.
    76→    async fn get_interaction(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Interaction>>;
    77→
    78→    /// Insert or update an interaction (upsert by ID).
    79→    async fn upsert_interaction(&self, interaction: Interaction) -> Result<()>;
    80→
    81→    /// Delete an interaction by ID. Returns `Ok(())` if not found (idempotent).
    82→    async fn delete_interaction(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()>;
    83→}

    85→pub use crate::{store_memory::MemoryCrmStore, store_sqlite::SqliteCrmStore};
    86→

1→use {async_trait::async_trait, secrecy::Secret, sqlx::SqlitePool};
     2→
     3→use crate::{
     4→    Error, Result,
     5→    store::CrmStore,
     6→    types::{
     7→        Contact, ContactChannel, ContactStage, Interaction, InteractionKind, Matter, MatterPhase,
     8→        MatterStatus, PracticeArea,
     9→    },
    10→};
    11→
    12→/// SQLite-backed implementation of [`CrmStore`].
    13→pub struct SqliteCrmStore {
    14→    pool: SqlitePool,
    15→}
    16→
    17→impl SqliteCrmStore {
    18→    pub fn new(pool: SqlitePool) -> Self {
    19→        Self { pool }
    20→    }
    21→}
    22→
    23→// ── Row types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24→
    25→#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]
    26→struct ContactRow {
    27→    id: String,
    28→    name: String,
    29→    source: Option<String>,
    30→    external_id: Option<String>,
    31→    email: Option<String>,
    32→    phone: Option<String>,
    33→    stage: String,
    34→    metadata: Option<String>,
    35→    created_at: i64,
    36→    updated_at: i64,
    37→}
    38→
    39→impl TryFrom<ContactRow> for Contact {
    40→    type Error = Error;
    41→
    42→    fn try_from(r: ContactRow) -> Result<Self> {
    43→        let stage = r
    44→            .stage
    45→            .parse::<ContactStage>()
    46→            .map_err(|_| Error::parse("contact.stage", &r.stage))?;
    47→        Ok(Contact {
    48→            id: r.id,
    49→            name: r.name,
    50→            source: r.source,
    51→            external_id: r.external_id,
    52→            email: r.email.map(Secret::new),
    53→            phone: r.phone.map(Secret::new),
    54→            stage,
    55→            metadata: serde_json::from_str(r.metadata.as_deref().unwrap_or("{}"))?,
    56→            created_at: r.created_at as u64,
    57→            updated_at: r.updated_at as u64,
    58→        })
    59→    }
    60→}
    61→
    62→#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]
    63→struct ContactChannelRow {
    64→    id: String,
    65→    contact_id: String,
    66→    channel_type: String,
    67→    channel_id: String,
    68→    display_name: Option<String>,
    69→    verified: i64,
    70→    created_at: i64,
    71→    updated_at: i64,
    72→}
    73→
    74→impl From<ContactChannelRow> for ContactChannel {
    75→    fn from(r: ContactChannelRow) -> Self {
    76→        Self {
    77→            id: r.id,
    78→            contact_id: r.contact_id,
    79→            channel_type: r.channel_type,
    80→            channel_id: r.channel_id,
    81→            display_name: r.display_name,
    82→            verified: r.verified != 0,
    83→            created_at: r.created_at as u64,
    84→            updated_at: r.updated_at as u64,
    85→        }
    86→    }
    87→}
    88→
    89→#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]
    90→struct MatterRow {
    91→    id: String,
    92→    contact_id: Option<String>,
    93→    title: String,
    94→    description: Option<String>,
    95→    status: String,
    96→    phase: String,
    97→    practice_area: String,
    98→    created_at: i64,
    99→    updated_at: i64,
   100→}
   101→
   102→impl TryFrom<MatterRow> for Matter {
   103→    type Error = Error;
   104→
   105→    fn try_from(r: MatterRow) -> Result<Self> {
   106→        let status = r
   107→            .status
   108→            .parse::<MatterStatus>()
   109→            .map_err(|_| Error::parse("matter.status", &r.status))?;
   110→        let phase = r
   111→            .phase
   112→            .parse::<MatterPhase>()
   113→            .map_err(|_| Error::parse("matter.phase", &r.phase))?;
   114→        let practice_area = r
   115→            .practice_area
   116→            .parse::<PracticeArea>()
   117→            .map_err(|_| Error::parse("matter.practice_area", &r.practice_area))?;
   118→        Ok(Matter {
   119→            id: r.id,
   120→            contact_id: r.contact_id,
   121→            title: r.title,
   122→            description: r.description,
   123→            status,
   124→            phase,
   125→            practice_area,
   126→            created_at: r.created_at as u64,
   127→            updated_at: r.updated_at as u64,
   128→        })
   129→    }
   130→}
   131→
   132→#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]
   133→struct InteractionRow {
   134→    id: String,
   135→    contact_id: String,
   136→    matter_id: Option<String>,
   137→    kind: String,
   138→    summary: String,
   139→    channel: Option<String>,
   140→    created_at: i64,
   141→    updated_at: i64,
   142→}
   143→
   144→impl TryFrom<InteractionRow> for Interaction {
   145→    type Error = Error;
   146→
   147→    fn try_from(r: InteractionRow) -> Result<Self> {
   148→        let kind = r
   149→            .kind
   150→            .parse::<InteractionKind>()
   151→            .map_err(|_| Error::parse("interaction.kind", &r.kind))?;
   152→        Ok(Interaction {
   153→            id: r.id,
   154→            contact_id: r.contact_id,
   155→            matter_id: r.matter_id,
   156→            kind,
   157→            summary: r.summary,
   158→            channel: r.channel,

   165→// ── CrmStore implementation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   166→
   167→#[async_trait]
   168→impl CrmStore for SqliteCrmStore {
   169→    // ── Contacts ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   170→
   171→    async fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<Contact>> {
   172→        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactRow>(
   173→            "SELECT id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, \
   174→             created_at, updated_at FROM crm_contacts ORDER BY updated_at DESC",
   175→        )
   176→        .fetch_all(&self.pool)
   177→        .await?;
   178→
   179→        rows.into_iter().map(Contact::try_from).collect()
   180→    }
   181→
   182→    async fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Contact>> {
   183→        let row = sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactRow>(
   184→            "SELECT id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, \
   185→             created_at, updated_at FROM crm_contacts WHERE id = ?",
   186→        )
   187→        .bind(id)
   188→        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
   189→        .await?;
   190→
   191→        row.map(Contact::try_from).transpose()
   192→    }
   193→
   194→    async fn get_by_external(&self, source: &str, external_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Contact>> {
   195→        let row = sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactRow>(
   196→            "SELECT id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, \
   197→             created_at, updated_at FROM crm_contacts WHERE source = ? AND external_id = ?",
   198→        )
   199→        .bind(source)
   200→        .bind(external_id)
   201→        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
   202→        .await?;
   203→
   204→        row.map(Contact::try_from).transpose()
   205→    }
   206→
   207→    async fn upsert(&self, contact: Contact) -> Result<()> {
   208→        use secrecy::ExposeSecret;
   209→        let metadata = serde_json::to_string(&contact.metadata)?;
   210→        sqlx::query(
   211→            r#"INSERT INTO crm_contacts
   212→               (id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, created_at, updated_at)
   213→               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   214→               ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET
   215→                 name        = excluded.name,
   216→                 source      = excluded.source,
   217→                 external_id = excluded.external_id,
   218→                 email       = excluded.email,
   219→                 phone       = excluded.phone,
   220→                 stage       = excluded.stage,
   221→                 metadata    = excluded.metadata,
   222→                 updated_at  = excluded.updated_at"#,
   223→        )
   224→        .bind(&contact.id)
   225→        .bind(&contact.name)
   226→        .bind(&contact.source)
   227→        .bind(&contact.external_id)
   228→        .bind(contact.email.as_ref().map(|s| s.expose_secret().clone()))
   229→        .bind(contact.phone.as_ref().map(|s| s.expose_secret().clone()))
   230→        .bind(contact.stage.as_str())
   231→        .bind(&metadata)
   232→        .bind(contact.created_at as i64)
   233→        .bind(contact.updated_at as i64)
   234→        .execute(&self.pool)
   235→        .await?;
   236→        Ok(())
   237→    }
   238→
   239→    async fn delete(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
   240→        sqlx::query("DELETE FROM crm_contacts WHERE id = ?")
   241→            .bind(id)
   242→            .execute(&self.pool)
   243→            .await?;
   244→        Ok(())
   245→    }
   246→
   247→    // ── Contact channels ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   248→
   249→    async fn list_channels_for_contact(&self, contact_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<ContactChannel>> {
   250→        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactChannelRow>(
   251→            "SELECT id, contact_id, channel_type, channel_id, display_name, verified, \
   252→             created_at, updated_at FROM crm_contact_channels \
   253→             WHERE contact_id = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC",
   254→        )
   255→        .bind(contact_id)
   256→        .fetch_all(&self.pool)
   257→        .await?;
   258→
   259→        Ok(rows.into_iter().map(ContactChannel::from).collect())
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    async fn get_channel_by_external(
   263→        &self,
   264→        channel_type: &str,
   265→        channel_id: &str,
   266→    ) -> Result<Option<ContactChannel>> {
   267→        let row = sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactChannelRow>(
   268→            "SELECT id, contact_id, channel_type, channel_id, display_name, verified, \
   269→             created_at, updated_at FROM crm_contact_channels \
   270→             WHERE channel_type = ? AND channel_id = ?",
   271→        )
   272→        .bind(channel_type)
   273→        .bind(channel_id)
   274→        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
   275→        .await?;
   276→
   277→        Ok(row.map(ContactChannel::from))
   278→    }
   279→
   280→    async fn upsert_channel(&self, channel: ContactChannel) -> Result<()> {
   281→        sqlx::query(
   282→            r#"INSERT INTO crm_contact_channels
   283→               (id, contact_id, channel_type, channel_id, display_name, verified, created_at, updated_at)
   284→               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   285→               ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET
   286→                 contact_id   = excluded.contact_id,
   287→                 channel_type = excluded.channel_type,
   288→                 channel_id   = excluded.channel_id,
   289→                 display_name = excluded.display_name,
   290→                 verified     = excluded.verified,
   291→                 updated_at   = excluded.updated_at"#,
   292→        )
   293→        .bind(&channel.id)
   294→        .bind(&channel.contact_id)
   295→        .bind(&channel.channel_type)
   296→        .bind(&channel.channel_id)
   297→        .bind(&channel.display_name)
   298→        .bind(channel.verified as i64)
   299→        .bind(channel.created_at as i64)
   300→        .bind(channel.updated_at as i64)
   301→        .execute(&self.pool)
   302→        .await?;
   303→        Ok(())
   304→    }
   305→
   306→    async fn delete_channel(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
   307→        sqlx::query("DELETE FROM crm_contact_channels WHERE id = ?")
   308→            .bind(id)
   309→            .execute(&self.pool)
   310→            .await?;
   311→        Ok(())
   312→    }

   352→    async fn upsert_matter(&self, matter: Matter) -> Result<()> {
   353→        sqlx::query(

   356→               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)

   463→// ── Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   464→
   465→#[cfg(test)]
   466→#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]
   467→mod tests {
   468→    use {
   469→        super::*,
   470→        crate::types::{
   471→            Contact, ContactChannel, Interaction, InteractionKind, Matter, PracticeArea,
   472→        },
   473→    };
   474→
   475→    async fn test_store() -> SqliteCrmStore {
   476→        let pool = SqlitePool::connect("sqlite::memory:").await.unwrap();
   477→        crate::run_migrations(&pool).await.unwrap();
   478→        SqliteCrmStore::new(pool)
   479→    }
   480→
   481→    // ── Contact tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   482→
   483→    #[tokio::test]
   484→    async fn upsert_and_get() {
   485→        let store = test_store().await;
   486→        let c = Contact::new("Alice");
   487→        let id = c.id.clone();
   488→        store.upsert(c).await.unwrap();
   489→        let found = store.get(&id).await.unwrap().unwrap();
   490→        assert_eq!(found.name, "Alice");
   491→    }
   492→
   493→    #[tokio::test]
   494→    async fn upsert_updates_existing() {
   495→        let store = test_store().await;
   496→        let mut c = Contact::new("Alice");
   497→        store.upsert(c.clone()).await.unwrap();
   498→        c.name = "Alice Updated".into();
   499→        store.upsert(c.clone()).await.unwrap();
   500→        let found = store.get(&c.id).await.unwrap().unwrap();
   501→        assert_eq!(found.name, "Alice Updated");
   502→    }
   503→
   504→    #[tokio::test]
   505→    async fn list_returns_all() {
   506→        let store = test_store().await;
   507→        store.upsert(Contact::new("A")).await.unwrap();
   508→        store.upsert(Contact::new("B")).await.unwrap();
   509→        let all = store.list().await.unwrap();
   510→        assert_eq!(all.len(), 2);
   511→    }
   512→
   513→    #[tokio::test]
   514→    async fn get_nonexistent_returns_none() {
   515→        let store = test_store().await;
   516→        assert!(store.get("missing-id").await.unwrap().is_none());
   517→    }
   518→
   519→    #[tokio::test]
   520→    async fn delete_is_idempotent() {
   521→        let store = test_store().await;
   522→        let c = Contact::new("X");
   523→        let id = c.id.clone();
   524→        store.upsert(c).await.unwrap();
   525→        store.delete(&id).await.unwrap();
   526→        store.delete(&id).await.unwrap();
   527→        assert!(store.get(&id).await.unwrap().is_none());
   528→    }

   530→    #[tokio::test]
   531→    async fn get_by_external_finds_contact() {
   532→        let store = test_store().await;
   533→        let c = Contact::with_source("Bob", "telegram", "99999");
   534→        store.upsert(c).await.unwrap();
   535→        let found = store
   536→            .get_by_external("telegram", "99999")
   537→            .await
   538→            .unwrap()
   539→            .unwrap();
   540→        assert_eq!(found.name, "Bob");

file.content:
-- CRM tables
-- Owned by: moltis-crm crate

-- ── crm_contacts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contacts (
    id          TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    name        TEXT    NOT NULL,
    source      TEXT,
    external_id TEXT,
    metadata    TEXT,
    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,
    updated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_contacts_updated_at
    ON crm_contacts(updated_at DESC);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_contacts_source_external_id
    ON crm_contacts(source, external_id)
    WHERE source IS NOT NULL AND external_id IS NOT NULL;

-- ── crm_matters ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Matters represent cases, deals, or topics being tracked for a contact.
-- contact_id is nullable: a matter may not be tied to a single contact.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_matters (
    id         TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    title      TEXT    NOT NULL,
    status     TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',
    contact_id TEXT    REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    metadata   TEXT,
    created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
    updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_matters_updated_at
    ON crm_matters(updated_at DESC);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_matters_contact_id
    ON crm_matters(contact_id)
    WHERE contact_id IS NOT NULL;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_matters_status
    ON crm_matters(status);

-- ── crm_interactions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Logged interactions (messages, calls, meetings, notes) with a contact.
-- contact_id is required; matter_id is optional.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_interactions (
    id         TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    contact_id TEXT    NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    matter_id  TEXT    REFERENCES crm_matters(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    kind       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    body       TEXT,
    metadata   TEXT,
    created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_interactions_contact_id
    ON crm_interactions(contact_id);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_interactions_matter_id
    ON crm_interactions(matter_id)
    WHERE matter_id IS NOT NULL;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_interactions_created_at
    ON crm_interactions(created_at DESC);

-- ── crm_contact_channels ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Maps contacts to communication channels (Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, …).
-- Each (channel_type, channel_identifier) pair belongs to exactly one contact.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contact_channels (
    id                 TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    contact_id         TEXT    NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    channel_type       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    channel_identifier TEXT    NOT NULL,
    verified           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE(channel_type, channel_identifier)
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_contact_channels_contact_id
    ON crm_contact_channels(contact_id);

   341→
   342→// ── Gateway state ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   343→
   344→/// Shared gateway runtime state, wrapped in `Arc` for use across async tasks.
   345→///
   346→/// Immutable fields and atomics live directly on this struct (no lock needed).
   347→/// All mutable runtime state is consolidated in [`GatewayInner`] behind a
   348→/// single `RwLock`.
   349→pub struct GatewayState {
   350→    // ── Immutable (set at construction, never changes) ──────────────────────
   351→    /// Server version string.
   352→    pub version: String,
   353→    /// Hostname for HelloOk.
   354→    pub hostname: String,
   355→    /// Auth configuration.
   356→    pub auth: ResolvedAuth,
   357→    /// Domain services.
   358→    pub services: GatewayServices,
   359→    /// Credential store for authentication (password, passkeys, API keys).
   360→    /// `Arc` because it is shared cross-crate (e.g. `ExecTool` as `dyn EnvVarProvider`).
   361→    pub credential_store: Option<Arc<CredentialStore>>,
   362→    /// Per-session sandbox router (None if sandbox is not configured).
   363→    /// `Arc` because it is shared with `ExecTool`/`ProcessTool` in `moltis-tools`.
   364→    pub sandbox_router: Option<Arc<SandboxRouter>>,
   365→    /// SQLite-backed pairing store for device token persistence.
   366→    /// `None` in tests that don't need pairing.
   367→    pub pairing_store: Option<Arc<PairingStore>>,
   368→    /// Memory manager for long-term memory search (None if no embedding provider).
   369→    /// `Arc` because it is cloned into background tokio tasks.
   370→    pub memory_manager: Option<Arc<moltis_memory::manager::MemoryManager>>,
   371→    /// Whether the server is bound to a loopback address (localhost/127.0.0.1/::1).
   372→    pub localhost_only: bool,
   373→    /// Whether the server is known to be behind a reverse proxy.
   374→    /// Set via `MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY=true`.  When true, loopback source IPs are
   375→    /// never treated as proof of a direct local connection.
   376→    pub behind_proxy: bool,
   377→    /// Whether TLS is active on the gateway listener.
   378→    pub tls_active: bool,
   379→    /// Whether WebSocket request/response logging is enabled.
   380→    pub ws_request_logs: bool,
   381→    /// Runtime GraphQL availability toggle.
   382→    #[cfg(feature = "graphql")]
   383→    pub graphql_enabled: AtomicBool,
   384→    /// Broadcast channel for GraphQL subscriptions. Events are `(event_name, payload)`.
   385→    #[cfg(feature = "graphql")]
   386→    pub graphql_broadcast: tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<(String, serde_json::Value)>,
   387→    /// Session event bus for cross-UI synchronisation (macOS ↔ web).
   388→    pub session_event_bus: SessionEventBus,
   389→    /// Cloud deploy platform (e.g. "flyio", "digitalocean"), read from
   390→    /// `MOLTIS_DEPLOY_PLATFORM`. `None` when running locally.
   391→    pub deploy_platform: Option<String>,
   392→    /// The port the gateway is bound to.
   393→    pub port: u16,
   394→    /// Monotonic process start timestamp used for uptime calculations.
   395→    pub started_at: Instant,
   396→    /// Metrics handle for Prometheus export (None if metrics disabled).
   397→    #[cfg(feature = "metrics")]
   398→    pub metrics_handle: Option<MetricsHandle>,
   399→    /// Persistent metrics store (SQLite or other backend).
   400→    #[cfg(feature = "metrics")]
   401→    pub metrics_store: Option<Arc<dyn MetricsStore>>,
   402→    /// Encryption-at-rest vault for environment variables.
   403→    #[cfg(feature = "vault")]
   404→    pub vault: Option<Arc<moltis_vault::Vault>>,
   405→
   406→    // ── Channel webhook deduplication (separate lock) ──────────────────────
   407→    /// Idempotency dedup store for channel webhooks. Uses its own
   408→    /// `std::sync::RwLock` to avoid contending with the main `inner` lock.
   409→    pub channel_webhook_dedup:
   410→        std::sync::RwLock<crate::channel_webhook_dedup::ChannelWebhookDedupeStore>,
   411→
   412→    /// Per-(channel, account) rate limiter for channel webhooks.
   413→    pub channel_webhook_rate_limiter: crate::channel_webhook_rate_limit::ChannelWebhookRateLimiter,
   414→
   415→    // ── Atomics (lock-free) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   416→    /// Monotonically increasing sequence counter for broadcast events.
   417→    pub seq: AtomicU64,
   418→    /// Sequential counter for TTS test phrase round-robin picking.
   419→    pub tts_phrase_counter: AtomicUsize,
   420→
   421→    // ── Mutable runtime state (single lock) ─────────────────────────────────
   422→    /// All mutable runtime state, behind a single lock.
   423→    pub inner: RwLock<GatewayInner>,
   424→}
   425→
   426→impl GatewayState {
   427→    pub fn new(auth: ResolvedAuth, services: GatewayServices) -> Arc<Self> {
   428→        Self::with_options(
   429→            auth,
   430→            services,
   431→            None,
   432→            None,
   433→            None,
   434→            false,
   435→            false,
   436→            false,

# Session Summary: Docker-in-Docker host data dir detection

- Added `host_data_dir` to sandbox config schema, validation, and generated config template.
- Updated sandbox mount resolution so Docker/Podman workspace mounts and default home persistence paths first auto-detect a host-visible `data_dir()` from the parent container's mounts, with `host_data_dir` as an explicit override.
- Added regression tests covering inspect parsing, mount translation, workspace mount overrides, and shared/session home persistence translation.

input.command:
cat <<'INVESTIGATION_EOF'
## Investigation

### Requirements

Issue CHA-13 asks to implement `CrmService` for the live store — meaning a service-traits-level `CrmService` trait and a `LiveCrmService` implementation in the gateway that wraps the existing `SqliteCrmStore`.

### Current State

The **`moltis-crm` crate** is already well-built with:
- **Domain types**: `Contact`, `ContactChannel`, `Matter`, `Interaction` plus enums (`ContactStage`, `MatterStatus`, `MatterPhase`, `PracticeArea`, `InteractionKind`)
- **`CrmStore` trait** (`store.rs`): Full async CRUD for all four entity types (contacts, channels, matters, interactions)
- **`SqliteCrmStore`** (`store_sqlite.rs`): Production SQLite implementation with upsert semantics, proper enum parsing, PII handling via `Secret<String>`
- **`MemoryCrmStore`** (`store_memory.rs`): In-memory implementation for testing
- **Migrations**: Two migration files creating all CRM tables with indexes and FK constraints
- **Error types** (`error.rs`): `thiserror`-based with `Sqlx`, `Migration`, `Json`, `NotFound`, `Parse`, `External` variants
- **Tests**: Comprehensive test suites for both memory and SQLite stores

The gateway already:
- Has `crm` as a default feature flag in `crates/gateway/Cargo.toml`
- Runs CRM migrations at startup (`server.rs:1873`)

**What's missing:**
1. No `CrmService` trait in `crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs`
2. No `LiveCrmService` struct in the gateway
3. No CRM entry in `GatewayServices`
4. No RPC handler routing `crm.*` methods
5. No REST/API routes for CRM

### Affected Files

| File | Change needed |
|------|--------------|
| `crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs` | Add `CrmService` trait + `NoopCrmService` default |
| `crates/gateway/src/services.rs` | Add `crm: Arc<dyn CrmService>` to `GatewayServices`, wire noop default |
| `crates/gateway/src/crm.rs` (new) | `LiveCrmService` wrapping `SqliteCrmStore`, implementing `CrmService` |
| `crates/gateway/src/server.rs` | Wire `LiveCrmService` after migrations, add to services |
| `crates/gateway/src/rpc.rs` or equivalent | Route `crm.*` RPC methods to the service |
| `crates/gateway/src/mod.rs` or `lib.rs` | Declare `mod crm` |

### Proposed Approach

1. **Define `CrmService` trait** in service-traits following existing patterns (e.g., `CronService`): async methods taking `Value` params, returning `ServiceResult`. Methods: `list_contacts`, `get_contact`, `upsert_contact`, `delete_contact`, `list_channels`, `upsert_channel`, `delete_channel`, `list_matters`, `get_matter`, `upsert_matter`, `delete_matter`, `list_interactions`, `get_interaction`, `upsert_interaction`, `delete_interaction`. Include `NoopCrmService`.

2. **Create `LiveCrmService`** in gateway wrapping `Arc<SqliteCrmStore>`. Each method deserialises params from `Value`, delegates to `CrmStore`, serialises results back to `Value`.

3. **Wire into `GatewayServices`**: Add `crm` field, initialise with `NoopCrmService` by default, swap for `LiveCrmService` in `prepare_gateway` (feature-gated on `crm`).

4. **Add RPC routing**: Register `crm.*` namespace in the gateway RPC dispatcher following the `cron.*` pattern.

5. **Tests**: Add integration tests for `LiveCrmService` using in-memory SQLite, verifying the `Value`-based interface end-to-end.

### Risks and Open Questions

- **PII in RPC responses**: Contact email/phone are `Secret<String>`. The service layer will need to decide whether to expose these in RPC results or redact them. Recommendation: expose them (they're already encrypted at rest and the RPC layer is auth-gated), but flag for review.
- **Config section**: CLAUDE.md mentions `crm_contacts`, `crm_matters`, etc. tables belong to `moltis-crm`. The recent CRM config section (CHA-7) may need to be checked for any CRM-specific settings that `LiveCrmService` should consume.
- **API namespace**: Per CLAUDE.md convention, CRM should get its own REST `/api/crm/...` namespace if REST endpoints are needed (beyond RPC). Scope decision: start with RPC only, add REST later.
INVESTIGATION_EOF

content.content:
1→use {async_trait::async_trait, secrecy::Secret, sqlx::SqlitePool};
     2→
     3→use crate::{
     4→    Error, Result,
     5→    store::CrmStore,
     6→    types::{
     7→        Contact, ContactChannel, ContactStage, Interaction, InteractionKind, Matter, MatterPhase,
     8→        MatterStatus, PracticeArea,
     9→    },
    10→};
    11→
    12→/// SQLite-backed implementation of [`CrmStore`].
    13→pub struct SqliteCrmStore {
    14→    pool: SqlitePool,
    15→}
    16→
    17→impl SqliteCrmStore {
    18→    pub fn new(pool: SqlitePool) -> Self {
    19→        Self { pool }
    20→    }
    21→}
    22→
    23→// ── Row types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24→
    25→#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]
    26→struct ContactRow {
    27→    id: String,
    28→    name: String,
    29→    source: Option<String>,
    30→    external_id: Option<String>,
    31→    email: Option<String>,
    32→    phone: Option<String>,
    33→    stage: String,
    34→    metadata: Option<String>,
    35→    created_at: i64,
    36→    updated_at: i64,
    37→}
    38→
    39→impl TryFrom<ContactRow> for Contact {
    40→    type Error = Error;
    41→
    42→    fn try_from(r: ContactRow) -> Result<Self> {
    43→        let stage = r
    44→            .stage
    45→            .parse::<ContactStage>()
    46→            .map_err(|_| Error::parse("contact.stage", &r.stage))?;
    47→        Ok(Contact {
    48→            id: r.id,
    49→            name: r.name,
    50→            source: r.source,
    51→            external_id: r.external_id,
    52→            email: r.email.map(Secret::new),
    53→            phone: r.phone.map(Secret::new),
    54→            stage,
    55→            metadata: serde_json::from_str(r.metadata.as_deref().unwrap_or("{}"))?,
    56→            created_at: r.created_at as u64,
    57→            updated_at: r.updated_at as u64,
    58→        })
    59→    }
    60→}
    61→
    62→#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]
    63→struct ContactChannelRow {
    64→    id: String,
    65→    contact_id: String,
    66→    channel_type: String,
    67→    channel_id: String,
    68→    display_name: Option<String>,
    69→    verified: i64,
    70→    created_at: i64,
    71→    updated_at: i64,
    72→}
    73→
    74→impl From<ContactChannelRow> for ContactChannel {
    75→    fn from(r: ContactChannelRow) -> Self {
    76→        Self {
    77→            id: r.id,
    78→            contact_id: r.contact_id,
    79→            channel_type: r.channel_type,
    80→            channel_id: r.channel_id,
    81→            display_name: r.display_name,
    82→            verified: r.verified != 0,
    83→            created_at: r.created_at as u64,
    84→            updated_at: r.updated_at as u64,
    85→        }
    86→    }
    87→}
    88→
    89→#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]
    90→struct MatterRow {
    91→    id: String,
    92→    contact_id: Option<String>,
    93→    title: String,
    94→    description: Option<String>,
    95→    status: String,
    96→    phase: String,
    97→    practice_area: String,
    98→    created_at: i64,
    99→    updated_at: i64,
   100→}
   101→
   102→impl TryFrom<MatterRow> for Matter {
   103→    type Error = Error;
   104→
   105→    fn try_from(r: MatterRow) -> Result<Self> {
   106→        let status = r
   107→            .status
   108→            .parse::<MatterStatus>()
   109→            .map_err(|_| Error::parse("matter.status", &r.status))?;
   110→        let phase = r
   111→            .phase
   112→            .parse::<MatterPhase>()
   113→            .map_err(|_| Error::parse("matter.phase", &r.phase))?;
   114→        let practice_area = r
   115→            .practice_area
   116→            .parse::<PracticeArea>()
   117→            .map_err(|_| Error::parse("matter.practice_area", &r.practice_area))?;
   118→        Ok(Matter {
   119→            id: r.id,
   120→            contact_id: r.contact_id,
   121→            title: r.title,
   122→            description: r.description,
   123→            status,
   124→            phase,
   125→            practice_area,
   126→            created_at: r.created_at as u64,
   127→            updated_at: r.updated_at as u64,
   128→        })
   129→    }
   130→}
   131→
   132→#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]
   133→struct InteractionRow {
   134→    id: String,
   135→    contact_id: String,
   136→    matter_id: Option<String>,
   137→    kind: String,
   138→    summary: String,
   139→    channel: Option<String>,
   140→    created_at: i64,
   141→    updated_at: i64,
   142→}
   143→
   144→impl TryFrom<InteractionRow> for Interaction {
   145→    type Error = Error;
   146→
   147→    fn try_from(r: InteractionRow) -> Result<Self> {
   148→        let kind = r
   149→            .kind
   150→            .parse::<InteractionKind>()
   151→            .map_err(|_| Error::parse("interaction.kind", &r.kind))?;
   152→        Ok(Interaction {
   153→            id: r.id,
   154→            contact_id: r.contact_id,
   155→            matter_id: r.matter_id,
   156→            kind,
   157→            summary: r.summary,
   158→            channel: r.channel,
   159→            created_at: r.created_at as u64,
   160→            updated_at: r.updated_at as u64,
   161→        })
   162→    }
   163→}
   164→
   165→// ── CrmStore implementation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   166→
   167→#[async_trait]
   168→impl CrmStore for SqliteCrmStore {
   169→    // ── Contacts ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   170→
   171→    async fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<Contact>> {
   172→        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactRow>(
   173→            "SELECT id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, \
   174→             created_at, updated_at FROM crm_contacts ORDER BY updated_at DESC",
   175→        )
   176→        .fetch_all(&self.pool)
   177→        .await?;
   178→
   179→        rows.into_iter().map(Contact::try_from).collect()
   180→    }
   181→
   182→    async fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Contact>> {
   183→        let row = sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactRow>(
   184→            "SELECT id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, \
   185→             created_at, updated_at FROM crm_contacts WHERE id = ?",
   186→        )
   187→        .bind(id)
   188→        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
   189→        .await?;
   190→
   191→        row.map(Contact::try_from).transpose()
   192→    }

   194→    async fn get_by_external(&self, source: &str, external_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Contact>> {
   195→        let row = sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactRow>(
   196→            "SELECT id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, \
   197→             created_at, updated_at FROM crm_contacts WHERE source = ? AND external_id = ?",
   198→        )
   199→        .bind(source)
   200→        .bind(external_id)
   201→        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
   202→        .await?;

   204→        row.map(Contact::try_from).transpose()

   206→
   207→    async fn upsert(&self, contact: Contact) -> Result<()> {
   208→        use secrecy::ExposeSecret;
   209→        let metadata = serde_json::to_string(&contact.metadata)?;
   210→        sqlx::query(
   211→            r#"INSERT INTO crm_contacts
   212→               (id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, created_at, updated_at)
   213→               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   214→               ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET
   215→                 name        = excluded.name,
   216→                 source      = excluded.source,
   217→                 external_id = excluded.external_id,
   218→                 email       = excluded.email,
   219→                 phone       = excluded.phone,
   220→                 stage       = excluded.stage,
   221→                 metadata    = excluded.metadata,
   222→                 updated_at  = excluded.updated_at"#,
   223→        )
   224→        .bind(&contact.id)
   225→        .bind(&contact.name)
   226→        .bind(&contact.source)
   227→        .bind(&contact.external_id)
   228→        .bind(contact.email.as_ref().map(|s| s.expose_secret().clone()))
   229→        .bind(contact.phone.as_ref().map(|s| s.expose_secret().clone()))
   230→        .bind(contact.stage.as_str())
   231→        .bind(&metadata)
   232→        .bind(contact.created_at as i64)
   233→        .bind(contact.updated_at as i64)
   234→        .execute(&self.pool)
   235→        .await?;
   236→        Ok(())
   237→    }
   238→
   239→    async fn delete(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
   240→        sqlx::query("DELETE FROM crm_contacts WHERE id = ?")
   241→            .bind(id)
   242→            .execute(&self.pool)
   243→            .await?;
   244→        Ok(())
   245→    }

cd /Users/samjp/Code/stokowski-workspaces/CHA-13

ISSUE_ID="d8862561-8a16-4a0a-bc54-a7d994ed6185"

python3 -c "
import urllib.request, json, os

api_key = os.environ['LINEAR_API_KEY']
issue_id = '$ISSUE_ID'

body = '''## Investigation

### Requirements

Issue CHA-13 asks to implement \`CrmService\` for the live store — a service-traits-level \`CrmService\` trait and a \`LiveCrmService\` implementation in the gateway that wraps the existing \`SqliteCrmStore\`.

### Current State

The **\`moltis-crm\` crate** is well-built with:
- **Domain types**: \`Contact\`, \`ContactChannel\`, \`Matter\`, \`Interaction\` plus enums (\`ContactStage\`, \`MatterStatus\`, \`MatterPhase\`, \`PracticeArea\`, \`InteractionKind\`)
- **\`CrmStore\` trait** (\`store.rs\`): Full async CRUD for all four entity types
- **\`SqliteCrmStore\`** (\`store_sqlite.rs\`): Production SQLite with upsert, enum parsing, PII via \`Secret<String>\`
- **\`MemoryCrmStore\`** (\`store_memory.rs\`): In-memory for testing
- **Migrations**: Two files creating all CRM tables with indexes and FK constraints
- **Error types**: \`thiserror\`-based with Sqlx, Migration, Json, NotFound, Parse, External
- **Tests**: Comprehensive suites for both stores

The gateway already:
- Has \`crm\` as a default feature in \`crates/gateway/Cargo.toml\` (\`crm = [\"dep:moltis-crm\"]\`)
- Runs CRM migrations at startup (\`server.rs:1873\`)
- Has \`CrmConfig\` in config schema (\`schema.rs:1055\`) with \`enabled\`, \`auto_create_contacts\`, \`default_practice_area\`

**What is missing:**
1. No \`CrmService\` trait in \`crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs\`
2. No \`LiveCrmService\` struct in the gateway
3. No CRM field in \`GatewayServices\` (\`services.rs:1221\`)
4. No RPC methods routing \`crm.*\` in \`methods/services.rs\`

### Affected Files

| File | Change needed |
|------|--------------|
| \`crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs\` | Add \`CrmService\` trait + \`NoopCrmService\` |
| \`crates/gateway/src/services.rs\` | Add \`crm: Arc<dyn CrmService>\` to \`GatewayServices\` |
| \`crates/gateway/src/crm_service.rs\` (new) | \`LiveCrmService\` wrapping \`SqliteCrmStore\` |
| \`crates/gateway/src/server.rs\` | Wire \`LiveCrmService\` after migrations |
| \`crates/gateway/src/methods/services.rs\` | Register \`crm.*\` RPC methods |
| \`crates/gateway/src/methods/mod.rs\` | Add \`crm.*\` to read/write method lists |
| \`crates/gateway/src/lib.rs\` | Declare \`mod crm_service\` |

### Proposed Approach

1. **Define \`CrmService\` trait** in service-traits following existing patterns (e.g. \`CronService\`): async methods taking \`Value\` params, returning \`ServiceResult\`. Methods: contacts CRUD, channels CRUD, matters CRUD, interactions CRUD. Include \`NoopCrmService\`.

2. **Create \`LiveCrmService\`** in gateway wrapping \`Arc<SqliteCrmStore>\`. Each method deserialises params from \`Value\`, delegates to \`CrmStore\`, serialises results. Gate behind \`CrmConfig.enabled\`.

3. **Wire into \`GatewayServices\`**: Add \`crm\` field, noop default, swap for live in \`prepare_gateway\` (feature-gated on \`crm\`).

4. **Add RPC routing**: Register \`crm.*\` namespace in \`methods/services.rs\` following the \`cron.*\` pattern. Add to read/write lists in \`methods/mod.rs\`.

5. **Tests**: Integration tests for \`LiveCrmService\` using in-memory SQLite.

### Risks and Open Questions

- **PII in RPC responses**: Contact email/phone are \`Secret<String>\`. Recommendation: expose them in RPC results (auth-gated), serialize via \`ExposeSecret\` at the service boundary.
- **CrmConfig.enabled**: The config has an \`enabled\` flag. Should \`LiveCrmService\` check this, or should the gateway skip wiring the live service entirely? Recommend: check at wire-up time in \`prepare_gateway\`.
- **API namespace**: Per CLAUDE.md, CRM should get REST \`/api/crm/...\` if needed. Recommend: start with RPC only, add REST later.
- **Config schema/validate sync**: CLAUDE.md requires \`build_schema_map()\` and \`check_semantic_warnings()\` updates when changing config. The CRM config already exists — verify it is in both.
'''

mutation = '''mutation($issueId: String!, $body: String!) {
  commentCreate(input: { issueId: \$issueId, body: \$body }) {
    success
    comment { id }
  }
}'''

payload = json.dumps({'query': mutation, 'variables': {'issueId': issue_id, 'body': body}})
req = urllib.request.Request(
    'https://api.linear.app/graphql',
    data=payload.encode(),
    headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'Authorization': api_key},
)
resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req)
data = json.loads(resp.read())
print('Investigation:', json.dumps(data, indent=2))
" 2>&1

# Global Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent running in a headless orchestration session.
There is no human in the loop — do not ask questions or wait for input.

## Ground rules

1. Read and follow the project's CLAUDE.md for coding conventions and standards.
2. Never use interactive commands, slash commands, or plan mode.
3. Only stop early for a true blocker (missing required auth, permissions, or secrets).
   If blocked, post the blocker details as a Linear comment and stop.
4. Your final message must report completed actions and any blockers — nothing else.

## Execution approach

- Spend extra effort on planning and verification.
- Read all relevant files before writing code.
- When planning: read CLAUDE.md, the existing code in the area you are modifying, and any related docs.
- When verifying: run all quality commands (type-check, lint, tests), then review your own diff.
- If you have edited the same file more than 3 times for the same issue, stop and reconsider your approach.

## Session startup

Before starting any implementation work:

1. Run the project's type-check command to verify the codebase compiles clean.
2. Run the project's test command to verify all tests pass.
3. If either fails, investigate and fix before starting new work.

## Linear workpad

Use a single Linear comment as a persistent workpad:

- Title: `## Workpad`
- Update it at each milestone with: current status, decisions made, and next steps.
- On rework runs, append the rework section — do not delete prior content.

## Rework awareness

Every prompt in this workflow serves both first-run and rework cases.
On rework runs, the workspace already contains prior work.  Check for:

- An existing feature branch (do not create a new one)
- An open PR (push to it, do not open a second)
- Review comments requesting changes (address them specifically)
- Prior workpad content (append to it, do not overwrite)

# Implementation Stage

You are implementing the solution for ****: 

**Current status:** 
**Labels:** 
**URL:** 

## Issue description


No description provided.


## Objective

Implement the solution, create a PR, and ensure it passes all quality checks.

> ## Investigation

### Requirements

Issue CHA-13 asks to implement `CrmService` for the live store — a service-traits-level `CrmService` trait and a `LiveCrmService` implementation in the gateway that wraps the existing `SqliteCrmStore`.

### Current State

The `moltis-crm` crate is well-built with:
- **Domain types**: `Contact`, `ContactChannel`, `Matter`, `Interaction` plus enums
- **`CrmStore` trait** (`store.rs`): Full async CRUD for all four entity types
- **`SqliteCrmStore`** (`store_sqlite.rs`): Production SQLite with upsert, enum parsing, PII via `Secret<String>`
- **`MemoryCrmStore`** (`store_memory.rs`): In-memory for testing
- **Migrations**: Two files creating all CRM tables with indexes and FK constraints
- **Tests**: Comprehensive suites for both stores

The gateway already:
- Has `crm` as a default feature (`crm = ["dep:moltis-crm"]`)
- Runs CRM migrations at startup (`server.rs:1873`)
- Has `CrmConfig` in config schema (`schema.rs:1055`) with `enabled`, `auto_create_contacts`, `default_practice_area`

**What is missing:**
1. No `CrmService` trait in `crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs`
2. No `LiveCrmService` struct in the gateway
3. No CRM field in `GatewayServices` (`services.rs:1221`)
4. No RPC methods routing `crm.*` in `methods/services.rs`

### Affected Files

| File | Change needed |
|------|--------------|
| `crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs` | Add `CrmService` trait + `NoopCrmService` |
| `crates/gateway/src/services.rs` | Add `crm: Arc<dyn CrmService>` to `GatewayServices` |
| `crates/gateway/src/crm_service.rs` (new) | `LiveCrmService` wrapping `SqliteCrmStore` |
| `crates/gateway/src/server.rs` | Wire `LiveCrmService` after migrations |
| `crates/gateway/src/methods/services.rs` | Register `crm.*` RPC methods |
| `crates/gateway/src/methods/mod.rs` | Add `crm.*` to read/write method lists |
| `crates/gateway/src/lib.rs` | Declare `mod crm_service` |

### Proposed Approach

1. **Define `CrmService` trait** in service-traits following `CronService` pattern: async methods taking `Value` params, returning `ServiceResult`. Methods: contacts CRUD, channels CRUD, matters CRUD, interactions CRUD. Include `NoopCrmService`.
2. **Create `LiveCrmService`** in gateway wrapping `Arc<SqliteCrmStore>`. Deserialize params from `Value`, delegate to `CrmStore`, serialize results. Gate behind `CrmConfig.enabled`.
3. **Wire into `GatewayServices`**: Add `crm` field, noop default, swap for live in `prepare_gateway` (feature-gated).
4. **Add RPC routing**: Register `crm.*` namespace in `methods/services.rs` following `cron.*` pattern.
5. **Tests**: Integration tests using in-memory SQLite.

### Risks and Open Questions

- **PII in RPC responses**: Contact email/phone are `Secret<String>`. Recommend: expose in auth-gated RPC, serialize via `ExposeSecret` at service boundary.
- **CrmConfig.enabled**: Recommend checking at wire-up time in `prepare_gateway`, not per-request.
- **API namespace**: Start with RPC only, add REST `/api/crm/...` later per CLAUDE.md convention.
- **Config validation**: Verify CRM config is already in `build_schema_map()` and `check_semantic_warnings()`.

- `complete` → **implementation-review**

# Global Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent running in a headless orchestration session.
There is no human in the loop — do not ask questions or wait for input.

## Ground rules

1. Read and follow the project's CLAUDE.md for coding conventions and standards.
2. Never use interactive commands, slash commands, or plan mode.
3. Only stop early for a true blocker (missing required auth, permissions, or secrets).
   If blocked, post the blocker details as a Linear comment and stop.
4. Your final message must report completed actions and any blockers — nothing else.

## Execution approach

- Spend extra effort on planning and verification.
- Read all relevant files before writing code.
- When planning: read CLAUDE.md, the existing code in the area you are modifying, and any related docs.
- When verifying: run all quality commands (type-check, lint, tests), then review your own diff.
- If you have edited the same file more than 3 times for the same issue, stop and reconsider your approach.

## Session startup

Before starting any implementation work:

1. Run the project's type-check command to verify the codebase compiles clean.
2. Run the project's test command to verify all tests pass.
3. If either fails, investigate and fix before starting new work.

## Linear workpad

Use a single Linear comment as a persistent workpad:

- Title: `## Workpad`
- Update it at each milestone with: current status, decisions made, and next steps.

- An existing feature branch (do not create a new one)
